title: Breitmann in Maryland
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Breitmann mit his gompany
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Rode out in Marylandt.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Dereן¿½s nix to trink in dis countrie;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ine droatן¿½s as dry as sand.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Itן¿½s light canteen und haversack,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Itן¿½s hoonger mixed mit doorst;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und if ve had some lager beer
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Iן¿½d trink oontil I boorst.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gling, glang, gloria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Veן¿½d trink oontil ve boorst.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Herr Leutן¿½nant, take a dozen men,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und ride dis land around!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Herr Feldwebel, go foraginן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dill somedings goot is found.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gotts-donder! men, go ploonder!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ve hafnן¿½t trinked a bit
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dis fourdeen hours! If I had beer
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Iן¿½d sauf oontil I shplit!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gling, glang, gloria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Veן¿½d sauf oontil ve shplit!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ At mitternacht a horseן¿½s hoofs
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Coom rattlinן¿½ droo de camp;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Rouse dere! coom rouse der house dere!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Herr Copitain ve moost tromp!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De scouds have found a repel town,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit repel davern near,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A repel keller in de cround,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit repel lager beer!!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gling, glang, gloria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ All fool of lager beer!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gottsdonnerkreuzschockschwerenoth!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How Breitmann broked de bush!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½O let me see dat lager beer!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O let me at him rush!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und is mein sabre sharp und true,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und is mein var-horse goot?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To get one quart of lager beer
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Iן¿½d shpill a sea of ploot.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gling, glang, gloria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Iן¿½d shpill a sea of ploot.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Fuenf hoonderd repels hold de down,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ One hoonderd strong are ve;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Who gares a tam for all de odds
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhen men so dirsty pe.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And in dey smashed and down dey crashed,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Like donder-polts dey fly,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Rash fort as der vild yן¿½ger cooms
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit blitzen droo de shky.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gling, glang, gloria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Like blitzen droo de shky.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How flewed to rite, how flewed to left
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De moundains, drees, und hedge;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How left und rite de yן¿½ger corps
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vent donderinן¿½ droo de pridge.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und splash und splosh dey ford de shtream
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere not some pridges pe:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ All dripplinן¿½ in de moondlight peam
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Stracks vent de Cavallrie.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gling, glang, gloria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Breitmannן¿½s cavallrie.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und hoory, hoory, on dey rote,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oonheedinן¿½ vet or try;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und horse und rider shnort and blowed
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und shparklinן¿½ bepples fly.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ropp! Ropp! I shmell de parley-prew!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½s somedings goot ish near.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ropp! Ropp! I scent de kneiperei;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Veן¿½ve got to lager beer!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gling, glang, gloria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Veן¿½ve got to lager beer!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hei! how de carpine pullets klinged
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oopon de helmets hart!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oh, Breitmann how dy sabre ringed;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Du alter Knasterbart!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De contrapands dey sing for shoy
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To see de rebs go down,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und hear der Breitmann grimly gry:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hoorah! veן¿½ve dook de down.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gling, glang, gloria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Victoria, victoria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De Dootch have dook de down.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mid shout and crash and sabre flash,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And vild husaren shout
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De Dootchmen boorst de keller in,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und rolled de lager out;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und in de coorlinן¿½ powder shmoke,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhile shtill de pullets sung,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere shtood der Breitmann, axe in hand,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A knockinן¿½ out de boong.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gling, glang, gloria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Victoria! Encoria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De shpicket beats de boong.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gotts! vot a shpree der Breitmann had
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhile yet his hand was red,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A trinkinן¿½ lager from his poots
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Among de repel tead.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½ן¿½Tvas dus dey vent at mitternight
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Along der moundain side;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Tvas dus dey help make history!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dis vas der Breitmannן¿½s ride.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gling, glang, gloria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Victoria! Victoria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Cerן¿½visia, encoria!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De treadful mitnight ride
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of Breitmannן¿½s vild Freischarlinger,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ All famous, broad, und vide.
meaning:
"Breitmann in Maryland" is a quintessential example of Charles G. Leland's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
